summary: hello244a postinstall exfiltrates environment and GitHub Actions OIDC token to remote webhook
details: package.json declares a postinstall script that runs an inline `node -e` payload on every `npm install`. The payload collects host information (os.platform, os.hostname, username) and the entire process.env, and — when running inside GitHub Actions — uses ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL and ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN to request an OIDC ID token from the runner. The collected environment plus the OIDC token are POSTed as JSON to the hardcoded endpoint https://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net/?cross_os_cloud=1. The command is wrapped with `|| true` to suppress errors so the install appears successful. Impact on installers: any secrets present in the environment at install time (CI tokens, cloud keys, npm/GitHub tokens) are exposed, and the stolen GitHub Actions OIDC token can be exchanged for AWS/GCP/Azure credentials via configured trust policies, giving the operator access to the installer's cloud infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
